subject: Terrible sewer smell coming up from my basement drain after every rain
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've lived at 4417 Hirsch Ave for eleven years and this spring is the worst I can remember. Every time we get more than a half inch of rain, this sulfur-like smell comes up through my basement floor drain for hours afterward. It's strong enough that I can't use the lower level. I called Public Works two weeks ago and was told to call the water department, who told me to call Public Works again. Can someone actually come out and look at what's happening with the main underneath Hirsch? This is a health issue, not just a nuisance.

subject: Terrible sewer smell coming up from my basement drain after every rain
body_clean: Ms. Tran, I pulled the inspection log for the Hirsch Ave corridor. The sanitary lateral serving that block was last rodded in October 2024. After heavy rain events we do see pressure surges that can push sewer gas back through floor drains — typically worse when the trap has dried out or when a backwater valve isn't installed. I'm scheduling a camera inspection of the main for the week of May 18th. In the meantime, pour a gallon of water down that floor drain to reseat the trap; it won't stop the root cause but it reduces the odor. We'll follow up once we have the camera footage.

subject: Terrible sewer smell coming up from my basement drain after every rain
body_clean: Mr. DiMeo, thank you for actually responding with specifics. I poured water down the drain the same evening and the smell is a bit less, but it came back strong after the rain on Thursday night. I'll be home all day May 19th if the camera crew needs access to the property. I'd really appreciate knowing what you find — and if there's a backwater valve program the Village runs, I'd like information on that as well.

subject: Neighbor regraded his yard and now my property floods
body_clean: Mayor, my name is Marcus Belfield, 3308 Granville Ave. My next-door neighbor at 3312 had landscapers come in about six weeks ago and raise the grade of his entire backyard — I'd estimate four to six inches higher than before. Now every rain event, and we've had three big ones since April, sends a sheet of water right over our shared property line and into my yard. My detached garage foundation is soaking. I've tried talking to him twice; he shrugs. Who in the Village handles a grading dispute like this? Is there an ordinance?

subject: Neighbor regraded his yard and now my property floods
body_clean: Mr. Belfield, thank you for contacting the Village. Bellwood Municipal Code Section 14-42 prohibits grading or earthwork that diverts stormwater onto an adjacent property. Based on your description, 3312 Granville may be in violation. I'll schedule a site inspection for this week and will request before-and-after grade measurements. If the violation is confirmed, the property owner at 3312 will receive a Notice of Violation with a 30-day compliance window to restore drainage to pre-disturbance conditions or install a swale that retains flow on-site. I'll contact you after the inspection with my findings.

subject: Catch basin at 25th and Washington still full of debris
body_clean: I reported this back in February when the snow started melting and I'm reporting it again now. The catch basin on the southwest corner of 25th Ave and Washington Blvd is packed with leaves, gravel and what looks like plastic sheeting. After the rain we had Monday, water sat in the intersection for nearly four hours. I had to walk my kids halfway around the block to get to school without soaking their shoes. I have photos if anyone wants them. This basin needed cleaning before spring and it still hasn't happened.

subject: Catch basin at 25th and Washington still full of debris
body_clean: Ms. Przybylski, you're right to push back on this. I checked our vactor truck schedule and that basin was on the list for the April sweep but got skipped when the truck went down for repairs. I'm moving it to the top of the priority queue — crew will be out Thursday morning, May 14th, weather permitting. If you have those photos, please send them to my attention at Public Works; they help us document backlog conditions for budget requests. I'm sorry about the runaround since February.

subject: Raw sewage smell near alley behind 2200 block of Rice Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, something is not right in the alley behind the 2200 block of Rice Ave. The smell started about ten days ago and got dramatically worse after the June 3rd storm. It smells like raw sewage, not just standing water. I've asked a few neighbors and two of them have noticed it too. One said she saw brown water seeping up from a crack in the alley pavement near the middle of the block the morning after the storm. This needs someone to come look right away — I'm worried about what kids might walk through back there.

subject: Raw sewage smell near alley behind 2200 block of Rice Ave
body_clean: Mr. Hutchins, thank you for flagging this — that description is serious and I want it addressed today, not next week. I've forwarded your message to Public Works Director DiMeo with a request for emergency inspection. If there is sanitary sewer compromise under that alley, we need to know the extent before any more rain. Please stay out of that alley in the meantime and let your neighbors know. I'll personally follow up with you once I hear back from his crew.

subject: Water pooling in my window well every time it rains
body_clean: Hello Mayor, I am at 709 Morris Ave and every spring the window well on the north side of my house fills completely with water. It has been this way for three years. This year it actually came through the window seal and got inside the wall. My neighbor across the alley mentioned the Village raised the alley grade when they repaved it in 2023 and water now runs toward our properties instead of away. I don't know if that's true but the flooding is worse since then. Is there any program that could help or at least someone who could look at the alley grade?

subject: Drainage pipe sticking out on Bohland Ave near the school
body_clean: Hi there. I walk past 4800 Bohland Ave every morning and there's a corrugated drainage pipe — maybe eight inches in diameter — that's been pushed out of the bank and is hanging about two feet over the sidewalk. It looks like it came loose during the spring thaw. It's low enough that a tall person would probably have to duck. Nobody's hit it yet as far as I know but it's a liability waiting to happen. Could someone from Public Works come out and look at this?

subject: Drainage pipe sticking out on Bohland Ave near the school
body_clean: Mr. Addams, I sent a crew by this afternoon. You're right — the end cap on a 10-inch CMP culvert shifted about 18 inches out of the headwall, probably from ice pressure. We've flagged it with safety tape for now and it's on tomorrow's repair list to reseat and pin the pipe. Should be corrected by end of day Wednesday. Thanks for catching that.

subject: Sump pump discharging onto my side yard from next door
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm hoping you can point me in the right direction. My neighbor at 1404 Madison St has his sump pump discharge hose running straight across his driveway and onto the parkway strip that borders my side yard. It runs constantly after rain and the ground on my side stays saturated for days. My hydrangeas are dying. I'm not trying to make trouble with the neighbor, but is there a Village rule about where sump discharge has to go? I'd rather have the Village explain it to him than have an argument over the fence.

subject: Sump pump discharging onto my side yard from next door
body_clean: Ms. Wierzbicki, you've described a situation that Code Enforcement can address directly. Village ordinance Section 14-38 requires that sump pump discharge be directed to a driveway, a splash block that drains to the street, or to an approved dry well — not onto an adjacent property. I will visit 1404 Madison St and speak with the owner about routing the discharge to the curb per Village standards. No complaint attribution is necessary; I'll handle it as a routine inspection. You should see a resolution within two to three weeks.

subject: Standing water at the dead end of St. Paul Ave — mosquito concern
body_clean: The dead end of St. Paul Ave near the railroad right-of-way has had standing water since the May storms. I'm talking about a low spot maybe 20 by 30 feet that just doesn't drain. It's been three weeks. At this point I'm more worried about mosquitoes and West Nile than the inconvenience. Is there a catch basin that's blocked back there, or is this a grading issue? My address is 2918 St. Paul Ave, about half a block from the problem. I haven't seen any Village equipment out there at all.

subject: Drainage problem behind Eastern Ave has gotten worse every year
body_clean: Mayor, I live at 408 Eastern Ave and the drainage behind our row of houses has been a slow-motion disaster. Each spring the water table is higher, the back alley stays wet longer, and the mold smell in my basement gets stronger. This spring after the April 22nd rain I had half an inch of water on my basement floor for the first time ever. I've called three times over the past two years and been told it's on the list. I understand budgets are tight but at what point does the Village accept that this is a structural drainage problem, not a routine maintenance issue?

subject: Drainage problem behind Eastern Ave has gotten worse every year
body_clean: Ms. Dupree, I hear you, and I want to be honest with you rather than give you another vague timeline. I'm asking Frank DiMeo in Public Works to pull every service record for the Eastern Ave corridor and give me a straight assessment of whether this is a maintenance backlog or a capital infrastructure problem that needs an engineering solution. You deserve a real answer, not a list position. I'll be back in touch within two weeks with something concrete. Thank you for your patience and for continuing to push on this.

subject: Regrading at construction site on 50th Ave pushing water toward my house
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. A contractor has been working on the lot at 4902 50th Ave — looks like new construction, maybe a garage addition. They've been pushing excavated dirt around and as best I can tell the grade along the south side of that lot now slopes toward my property at 4906. We had a 1.2-inch rain last Tuesday and I had water running along my foundation for the first time since I bought this house. Are they required to have an erosion or drainage plan on file? Is there someone I can call to check if what they're doing is permitted?

subject: Regrading at construction site on 50th Ave pushing water toward my house
body_clean: Mr. Osei, I checked the permit file for 4902 50th Ave. There is an approved permit for a detached garage, but the approved grading plan does not authorize the earthwork you're describing on the south side. I visited the site this afternoon and the soil stockpile is indeed directing runoff toward your property. I've issued a Stop Work Order for grading activities until the contractor submits a revised drainage plan for review. The permit holder has been notified. Please document any further foundation water intrusion with dated photos.

subject: Flooded viaduct on Mannheim Rd after June 3rd storm
body_clean: Mayor, I know Mannheim is a state road but I'm writing to you because I don't know who else to contact. The underpass at Mannheim Rd near the Metra tracks was impassable for at least six hours after the June 3rd storm — water halfway up the curb, cars turning around. I saw one SUV try to push through and stall out. The drainage pump under there seems to have failed or is undersized. Does the Village have any direct contact with IDOT District 1 that could get this escalated? It's a safety issue, not just an inconvenience.

subject: Flooded viaduct on Mannheim Rd after June 3rd storm
body_clean: Mr. Flemming, you're correct that Mannheim Road is IDOT jurisdiction, but that doesn't mean we sit on our hands. I've already spoken with our state legislative contact and I'm drafting a formal letter to IDOT District 1 documenting the June 3rd pump failure and requesting an engineering review of the underpass drainage capacity. I'd appreciate any photographs you have from that morning — dated phone photos work fine. Our Village Engineer will co-sign the letter to give it more technical weight. I'll loop you in when we have a response from Springfield.

subject: Thank you — catch basin on Frederick Ave finally cleaned
body_clean: Just a quick note to say thank you. The catch basin on Frederick Ave near my house at 3715 has been a problem for two years and last week I saw the vactor truck out there for a good hour. I was out of town and came home to a dry curb lane even after yesterday's rain. That's the first time that's happened in recent memory. I know these things can feel thankless so I wanted to make sure someone heard that it made a real difference to the neighborhood. Please pass along my appreciation to whoever on the crew made it happen.
